data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I fork upstream + verify repo accessibility + confirm org write access
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create the public GitHub fork `navapbc/digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template` from upstream `navapbc/template-application-nextjs`, verify accessibility, and confirm the executing principal has write access to the navapbc org.
**Why**: Walking skeleton first step — all downstream stories modify this fork; without it, no work can land.
**Scope**:
- IN: `gh repo fork` (or equivalent); verify public visibility; confirm upstream Apache-2.0 LICENSE carryforward; confirm navapbc org write permissions.
- OUT: Copier rendering; nava-platform stripping; DSO infrastructure; placeholders.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `gh repo view navapbc/digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template --json visibility` returns `public`.
  ← Satisfies: "The public repo https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template exists, is public, clones without authentication"
- When this story is complete, upstream `LICENSE` (Apache-2.0) is preserved unmodified in the fork's default branch.
  ← Satisfies: "Apache-2.0 §4 attribution is satisfied: upstream LICENSE preserved unmodified"
- When this story is complete, the executing principal has confirmed write access to the `navapbc` GitHub org.
  ← Satisfies: "Prerequisite (must verify before sprint work begins): Write access to the navapbc GitHub org is confirmed"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] External dependency on GitHub org permissions — if access is denied, the epic blocks and falls back to the Phase 1 repo-location question.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
